body.title: [in_progress] Mark the anthropic inference source zero-balance/unavailable (browser-auth, no key)
body.intention: I intend that the inference proxy knows paid Anthropic is OFF — zero balance, no API key (Akien moved to a 5x Max plan with browser auth) — so the cost-optimizing router never selects the anthropic source and correctly reflects that only free/owned-local tiers plus a small OpenRouter balance remain.
body.description:
Akien 2026-07-03: now on a 5x Max plan, logged in via browser — NO API keys. The AnthropicSource (unseen_university/devices/inference/sources.py:367, checks ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/REAL_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY then akien.credentials.cfg, else raises) would fail or bill nothing. Mark anthropic as zero-balance/unavailable in the proxy's source info so the router (rules_engine.py / models_registry.py cost+capability data) does not select it. Note OpenRouter has ~$20 — Akien will re-enable it later to burn+test, and will NOT top up; so OR stays available-but-finite, anthropic goes unavailable.

Discovery: find WHERE source availability/balance/cost lives (models_registry.py, rules_engine.py source registration, or a cost/capability data file) and set anthropic accordingly — a data change, not a new mechanism (per [[project_inference_cost_optimizing_router]] — complexity lives in the data).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/ (sources.py registration and/or models_registry.py / rules_engine.py source-availability data — discovery in sprint).
**Design rules:** inference-proxy-only (all inference through the proxy); tier-not-model contract.
**Scope boundary:** IN — mark anthropic source zero-balance/unavailable in the source info so the router skips it; keep OR available-but-finite. OUT — removing the AnthropicSource class (keep it, just unavailable — browser-auth CC doesn't route through the proxy anyway); changing routing mechanism.
**Test plan:** unit — the router/selector does NOT return anthropic for any tier while it's marked unavailable (red: anthropic selected/raises; green: skipped with a log line naming it unavailable). Integration against the real registry.
**Completion criteria:** the router never selects anthropic while it's marked zero-balance/unavailable, and logs the skip; OR remains selectable.
**Rollback:** flip the anthropic source-availability data back.
